text: Entire frame black; no visible lecture content, text, diagrams, handwriting, slides, or objects.

Aerial landscape of rolling mountain hills and ridges covered in dense spring wildflower bloom. Large irregular patches of bright yellow flowers dominate many slopes; additional smaller patches of orange and purple flowers scattered across hillsides. Gray and tan rocky outcrops and bare soil bands visible between vegetated areas. Green shrubs and grasses cover much of the terrain, forming alternating stripes and mottled textures along the contours of the hills. Foreground and midground: closely spaced ridges with repeating wave-like folds. Background: higher rounded hills extending across the frame. Top edge: narrow strip of pale blue sky. No lecture text, labels, diagrams, or other academic annotations visible.

No lecture text or diagrams.

Panoramic landscape of layered mountains/hills under a pale blue sky. Slopes covered in irregular broad patches of bright yellow vegetation interspersed with green areas and exposed gray rocky bands/ridges. Repeating folded, striped terrain patterns across the foreground and midground, with yellow bands tracing contours of the hills. A few small orange-red patches near the upper central slopes. Distant rounded ridgelines extend across the full width of the frame.

Computer desktop wallpaper only; no lecture text, diagrams, or labels in the main image.

Main content:
- Aerial landscape of folded mountain ridges and valleys.
- Bright yellow-green vegetation covering many slopes and ridge crests.
- Gray-white exposed rock bands and striated rocky faces alternating with vegetation.
- Darker green patches of shrubs/trees scattered across slopes and valley bottoms.
- Small areas of orange-red vegetation near upper central ridges.
- Repeating zigzag / chevron-like ridge patterns across foreground and midground.
- Background: more rounded green hills under a pale blue sky.

Academic visual content:
- Repeated anticline/syncline-like ridge patterns across entire frame.
- Exposed rock layers in gray, tan, brown, and muted purple tones.
- Extensive bright yellow vegetation/flower cover spread irregularly over many slopes and ridge crests.
- Patches of green vegetation intermixed with yellow, concentrated in valleys and along some ridge lines.
- Small red/orange patches near upper central hills.
- Foreground: multiple parallel, tightly folded ridges with alternating yellow-covered and gray rocky bands.
- Middle ground: broad central hill with large yellow patch; surrounding gray eroded slopes with visible linear striations/gullies.
- Background: continuous line of rounded hills across horizon, mottled green and yellow.
- Sky: narrow pale blue strip above horizon.

Lower-left figure block:

- vertical stack of small rectangular trace panels, each with a sinusoidal or oscillatory waveform of different frequency

  - “Gamma >30 Hz”
  - “Beta (around 20 Hz)”
  - “Alpha (around 10 Hz)”
  - “Theta (around 6 Hz)”
  - “Delta (around 1– 2 Hz)”

Layout/content meaning:
- slide contrasts TMS with tES
- tES subdivided into tDCS and tACS
- tACS associated with enhancing endogenous brain oscillations across canonical frequency bands gamma, beta, alpha, theta, delta
